# Story 1.12: Landing Page, Artist Route & Not-Found Pages

## Status

review

## Story

As a music lover,
I want an instantly-loaded landing graph and direct URL access to any artist,
So that Dig's first impression is immediate and every graph state is shareable from the start.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** `public/data/miles-davis.json` is generated by running `graph-builder.ts` against Miles Davis's live MBID
**When** I inspect the file
**Then** it exactly conforms to the `GraphData` TypeScript interface
**And** it was NOT hand-crafted — it was produced by `graph-builder` to guarantee structural consistency
**And** it contains at least one visible cross-genre connection accessible without pivoting (the aha moment)

**Given** `src/app/page.tsx` (landing page) is implemented
**When** I load localhost:3000
**Then** the Miles Davis graph appears within 1 second — no API call, no loading state
**And** "Follow the thread." appears as subtitle copy beneath the search bar

**Given** `src/app/artist/[slug]/page.tsx` is implemented
**When** I navigate to `/artist/radiohead`
**Then** `resolveSlug('radiohead')` resolves to Radiohead's MBID
**And** `useArtistGraph(mbid)` triggers and the graph loads progressively
**And** Zustand `focalArtistId` is set to Radiohead's MBID

**Given** an unknown slug is navigated to (e.g., `/artist/xxxxunknown`)
**When** resolution fails
**Then** `src/app/artist/[slug]/not-found.tsx` renders: search bar prominent, text "We couldn't find that artist. Try searching above."
**And** HTTP status is 404 (not 200 or 500)
**And** the user can search from the not-found page

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create `scripts/generate-landing-data.ts` and generate `public/data/miles-davis.json` (AC: 1)
  - [x] 1.1 If `tsx` is not a devDependency, add it: `npm install --save-dev tsx`
  - [x] 1.2 Create `scripts/` directory and `scripts/generate-landing-data.ts` — imports `buildGraph` from `@/lib/data/graph-builder` (tsx resolves `@/` via root tsconfig; fall back to relative `../src/lib/data/graph-builder` if module-not-found errors occur)
  - [x] 1.3 MILES_DAVIS_MBID constant in the script: `"561d854a-6a28-4aa7-8c99-323e6ce46c2a"`
  - [x] 1.4 Script calls `buildGraph(MILES_DAVIS_MBID, 2)` and writes `JSON.stringify(graphData, null, 2)` to `public/data/miles-davis.json` using Node.js `fs.writeFileSync` (no streaming needed for a JSON file of this size)
  - [x] 1.5 Add `"generate:landing": "npx tsx scripts/generate-landing-data.ts"` to `package.json` scripts section
  - [x] 1.6 Run `npm run generate:landing` — script makes live MusicBrainz API calls; first run takes **30–60 seconds** due to the 1 req/sec rate limiter in `musicbrainz.ts`. Do NOT interrupt or retry early.
  - [x] 1.7 Verify `public/data/miles-davis.json` is valid JSON, has a `focalArtist`, `artists`, `edges`, `depth`, `warnings` shape (matches `GraphData` interface), and `edges.length > 0`
  - [x] 1.8 Commit `public/data/miles-davis.json` — this file must be in the repository; it is the sole data source for the landing page

- [x] Task 2: Update `src/app/page.tsx` — static landing with no loading state (AC: 2)
  - [x] 2.1 **PREREQUISITE**: `public/data/miles-davis.json` must exist (Task 1 complete) before adding the import below — TypeScript/build will error if the file is missing
  - [x] 2.2 Add static JSON import at module level (outside component): `import milesDataRaw from "@/../public/data/miles-davis.json"` and cast: `const milesData = milesDataRaw as GraphData` (tsconfig already has `resolveJsonModule: true`)
  - [x] 2.3 Remove the `activeMbid` constant: change `useArtistGraph(activeMbid)` → `useArtistGraph(focalArtistId)` — when `focalArtistId === null`, the query is **disabled** via `enabled: !!mbid` in the hook; no API call is made
  - [x] 2.4 Update `graphData` computation: `const graphData: GraphData | null = focalArtistId ? (data?.data ?? null) : milesData` — static data on landing (null store), live data after any pivot or search
  - [x] 2.5 Guard loading/error states behind `focalArtistId`: change `if (isPending)` → `if (focalArtistId && isPending)` and `if (error)` → `if (focalArtistId && error)` — the landing page always has `milesData` immediately (no loading spinner on first visit)
  - [x] 2.6 Add "Follow the thread." subtitle — render only when `focalArtistId === null`
  - [x] 2.7 Keep `MILES_DAVIS_MBID` constant and the `history.replaceState` `useEffect` unchanged — the constant is still needed for the initial history state (no longer used as `useArtistGraph` argument)
  - [x] 2.8 Remove the `MILES_DAVIS_MBID` fallback from `useArtistGraph` argument — that was the pre-1.12 temporary approach

- [x] Task 3: Create `src/app/artist/[slug]/page.tsx` — Server Component entry point (AC: 3)
  - [x] 3.1 Server Component — NO `"use client"` directive; default export required
  - [x] 3.2 Function signature: `export default async function ArtistPage({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> })` — Next.js 16 passes `params` as a `Promise`; TypeScript will error if you destructure without awaiting
  - [x] 3.3 `const { slug } = await params` — must be awaited at the top of the function body
  - [x] 3.4 Resolve slug to MBID with try/catch: ArtistNotFoundError → notFound(); other errors rethrown

  - [x] 4.2 Sync Zustand store on mount/change: `useEffect(() => { setFocalArtist(mbid); }, [mbid, setFocalArtist])`
  - [x] 4.3 `const { data, isPending, error } = useArtistGraph(mbid)` — mbid always a non-null string here

  - [x] 4.5 `handlePivot` derives slug from graphData artists, uses `router.push` — setFocalArtist NOT called

## Dev Notes

### File locations (Next.js App Router structure)

```
src/app/
  page.tsx                          ← MODIFY (Task 2)
  not-found.tsx                     ← NEW (Task 6) — global 404
  artist/
    [slug]/
      page.tsx                      ← NEW (Task 3) — Server Component
      ArtistGraphView.tsx           ← NEW (Task 4) — Client Component
      not-found.tsx                 ← NEW (Task 5) — route-level 404

scripts/
  generate-landing-data.ts          ← NEW (Task 1)

public/
  data/
    miles-davis.json                ← NEW (Task 1, generated + committed)
```

No `index.tsx` directory pattern — single file per component per architecture.

### Named vs default exports — Next.js App Router rule

Architecture mandates named exports for ALL component files. Exception: Next.js App Router **route files** that Next.js requires to be default exports:

| File | Export | Why |
|------|--------|-----|
| `src/app/page.tsx` | `export default function Home()` | Next.js page requirement |
| `src/app/not-found.tsx` | `export default function NotFound()` | Next.js page requirement |
| `src/app/artist/[slug]/page.tsx` | `export default async function ArtistPage()` | Next.js page requirement |
| `src/app/artist/[slug]/not-found.tsx` | `export default function NotFound()` | Next.js page requirement |
| `src/app/artist/[slug]/ArtistGraphView.tsx` | `export function ArtistGraphView()` | Component file — named only |

### Generate script — tsx and import paths

The script runs outside Next.js via `npx tsx`. tsx resolves TypeScript path aliases via `tsconfig.json` when the tsconfig is in the project root (which it is). The `@/` alias should work.

If you encounter "Cannot find module '@/lib/data/graph-builder'" errors, fall back to relative imports:
```typescript
// Relative import fallback (from scripts/ directory)
import { buildGraph } from "../src/lib/data/graph-builder";
import { writeFileSync } from "fs";
```

Script boilerplate:
```typescript
// scripts/generate-landing-data.ts
import { buildGraph } from "@/lib/data/graph-builder";
import { writeFileSync } from "fs";
import { join } from "path";

const MILES_DAVIS_MBID = "561d854a-6a28-4aa7-8c99-323e6ce46c2a";
const OUTPUT_PATH = join(process.cwd(), "public", "data", "miles-davis.json");

async function main() {
  console.log("Generating landing data for Miles Davis…");
  const graphData = await buildGraph(MILES_DAVIS_MBID, 2);
  writeFileSync(OUTPUT_PATH, JSON.stringify(graphData, null, 2));
  console.log(`Written to ${OUTPUT_PATH}`);
  console.log(`Artists: ${graphData.artists.length}, Edges: ${graphData.edges.length}`);
}

main().catch((err) => {
  console.error(err);
  process.exit(1);
});
```

**Rate limiting**: `graph-builder.ts` calls `searchArtists` from `musicbrainz.ts`, which enforces 1 req/sec. For Miles Davis with depth=2, expect 30–60 API calls → **30–60 second runtime**. This is expected and correct.

**First-run requirement**: The script must be run before implementing Task 2. The import statement in `page.tsx` will fail at TypeScript compile time if `public/data/miles-davis.json` does not exist.

### Static JSON import pattern

`tsconfig.json` already has `"resolveJsonModule": true`. No tsconfig changes needed.

```typescript
// src/app/page.tsx — add at module level, OUTSIDE component function
import milesDataRaw from "@/../public/data/miles-davis.json";
import type { GraphData } from "@/lib/data/types";

// Type assertion needed: TypeScript infers a deeply nested literal type from JSON imports
const milesData = milesDataRaw as GraphData;
```

Path resolution: `@/` = `./src/` → `@/../` = `./` (project root) → `@/../public/data/miles-davis.json` = `./public/data/miles-davis.json` ✅

### Landing page graphData flow — critical logic

The pre-Story-1.12 pattern used `focalArtistId ?? MILES_DAVIS_MBID` as the `useArtistGraph` argument, always making an API call. Story 1.12 replaces this with static JSON on landing.

```typescript
// src/app/page.tsx — GraphView function body (after Story 1.12)

function GraphView() {
  const setFocalArtist = useDigStore((state) => state.setFocalArtist);
  const focalArtistId = useDigStore((state) => state.focalArtistId);

  // Query disabled when focalArtistId is null (enabled: !!mbid in the hook)
  const { data, isPending, error } = useArtistGraph(focalArtistId);

  // CRITICAL: Static data on landing; live data after any pivot or search
  const graphData: GraphData | null = focalArtistId
    ? (data?.data ?? null)
    : milesData;

  // ...handleArtistSelect and handlePivot callbacks (unchanged from Story 1.11)...

  // Only show loading/error states when a live query is in flight
  if (focalArtistId && isPending) {
    return (
      <>
        <TopNav onArtistSelect={handleArtistSelect} />
        <div className="flex h-full items-center justify-center text-[#A09880]">
          Loading…
        </div>
      </>
    );
  }

  if (focalArtistId && error) {
    return (
      <>
        <TopNav onArtistSelect={handleArtistSelect} />
        <div className="flex h-full items-center justify-center text-[#A09880]">
          Error: {error.message}
        </div>
      </>
    );
  }

  // graphData is guaranteed non-null here:
  // - Landing (focalArtistId null): graphData = milesData (static, always present)
  // - Post-search/pivot (focalArtistId set): live query succeeded (guards above passed)
  return (
    <>
      <TopNav onArtistSelect={handleArtistSelect} />
      {!focalArtistId && (
        <p className="fixed top-12 left-0 right-0 text-center text-[13px] text-[#A09880] pointer-events-none z-40 select-none">
          Follow the thread.
        </p>
      )}
      <GraphCanvas
        graphData={graphData}
        filterEras={[]}
        filterGenres={[]}
        onPivot={handlePivot}
        focalArtistName={graphData?.focalArtist.name ?? ""}
        upstreamCount={graphData ? countByDirection(graphData, "upstream") : 0}
        downstreamCount={graphData ? countByDirection(graphData, "downstream") : 0}
      />
    </>
  );
}
```

### Next.js 16 Server Component — artist route page

```typescript
// src/app/artist/[slug]/page.tsx
import { notFound } from "next/navigation";
import { resolveSlug } from "@/lib/data/slugs";
import { ArtistNotFoundError } from "@/lib/errors";
import { ArtistGraphView } from "./ArtistGraphView";

export default async function ArtistPage({
  params,
}: {
  params: Promise<{ slug: string }>; // Next.js 16: params is a Promise
}) {
  const { slug } = await params; // MUST await before destructuring

  let mbid: string;
  try {
    mbid = await resolveSlug(slug);
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof ArtistNotFoundError) {
      notFound(); // TypeScript return type is `never` — code stops here
    }
    throw error; // Other errors (DataSourceError) → error boundary (500)
  }

  return <ArtistGraphView mbid={mbid} />;
}
```

**Why `let mbid` before the try block**: TypeScript knows `notFound()` returns `never`, so after the catch branch that calls it, control never continues. TypeScript is satisfied that `mbid` is assigned before the `return` statement.

### ArtistGraphView — navigation pattern

`ArtistGraphView` uses `router.push()` for both search selection and node pivot, unlike `page.tsx` which uses `window.history.pushState`. The difference:
- `window.history.pushState` (landing page): updates URL without a full navigation; stays on the same page component, just refetches data
- `router.push()` (artist route): triggers Next.js client-side navigation to the new slug's `page.tsx` Server Component

This is correct behavior: on the landing page, the graph updates in-place; on the artist route, each pivot navigates to a new route (the Server Component runs `resolveSlug` again).

**`setFocalArtist` ownership**: In `ArtistGraphView`, do NOT call `setFocalArtist` in `handleArtistSelect` or `handlePivot`. The new `ArtistGraphView` instance (rendered by the next route) will call `setFocalArtist(mbid)` via `useEffect` on mount. Calling it in both places causes a double update and potential flicker.

```typescript
// src/app/artist/[slug]/ArtistGraphView.tsx
"use client";

import { useCallback, useEffect } from "react";
import { useRouter } from "next/navigation"; // ← App Router (NOT next/router)
import { useArtistGraph } from "@/hooks/useArtistGraph";
import { GraphCanvas } from "@/graph/GraphCanvas";
import { GraphErrorBoundary } from "@/graph/GraphErrorBoundary";
import { TopNav } from "@/components/nav/TopNav";
import { useDigStore } from "@/store";
import type { Artist, GraphData } from "@/lib/data/types";

function countByDirection(graphData: GraphData, dir: "upstream" | "downstream") {
  return graphData.edges.filter((e) => e.direction === dir).length;
}

export function ArtistGraphView({ mbid }: { mbid: string }) {
  const router = useRouter();
  const setFocalArtist = useDigStore((state) => state.setFocalArtist);
  const { data, isPending, error } = useArtistGraph(mbid);
  const graphData = data?.data ?? null;

  // Sync Zustand so any hooks that read focalArtistId see the current artist
  useEffect(() => {
    setFocalArtist(mbid);
  }, [mbid, setFocalArtist]);

  const handleArtistSelect = useCallback(
    (artist: Artist) => {
      router.push(`/artist/${artist.slug}`);
    },
    [router],
  );

  const handlePivot = useCallback(
    (pivotMbid: string) => {
      const artist = graphData?.artists.find((a) => a.mbid === pivotMbid);
      if (artist?.slug) {
        router.push(`/artist/${artist.slug}`);
      }
    },
    [graphData, router],
  );

  if (isPending) { /* ... loading UI ... */ }
  if (error) { /* ... error UI ... */ }

  return (
    <GraphErrorBoundary fallback={/* ... */}>
      <TopNav onArtistSelect={handleArtistSelect} />
      <GraphCanvas
        graphData={graphData}
        filterEras={[]}
        filterGenres={[]}
        onPivot={handlePivot}
        focalArtistName={graphData?.focalArtist.name ?? ""}
        upstreamCount={graphData ? countByDirection(graphData, "upstream") : 0}
        downstreamCount={graphData ? countByDirection(graphData, "downstream") : 0}
      />
    </GraphErrorBoundary>
  );
}
```

### not-found.tsx — TopNav search and navigation

```tsx
// src/app/artist/[slug]/not-found.tsx
"use client";
import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";
import { TopNav } from "@/components/nav/TopNav";
import type { Artist } from "@/lib/data/types";

export default function NotFound() {
  const router = useRouter();

  function handleArtistSelect(artist: Artist) {
    router.push(`/artist/${artist.slug}`);
  }

  return (
    <div className="h-full flex flex-col bg-canvas">
      <TopNav onArtistSelect={handleArtistSelect} />
      <div className="flex flex-1 items-center justify-center">
        <p className="text-[15px] text-[#A09880] text-center px-4">
          We couldn&apos;t find that artist. Try searching above.
        </p>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

### resolveSlug — error contract (already implemented)

```typescript
// src/lib/data/slugs.ts — DO NOT modify
export async function resolveSlug(slug: string): Promise<string>
// Returns: MBID string
// Throws: ArtistNotFoundError when no artist matches the slug

import { ArtistNotFoundError } from "@/lib/errors"; // correct import path
```

### Zustand selector pattern — mandatory

```typescript
// ✅ Correct — selector function, subscribes to single slice
const setFocalArtist = useDigStore((state) => state.setFocalArtist);
const focalArtistId = useDigStore((state) => state.focalArtistId);

// ❌ Wrong — subscribes to entire store, triggers on every update
const { setFocalArtist } = useDigStore();
```

### useRouter import — correct source

```typescript
// ✅ App Router — correct for this project
import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";

// ❌ Pages Router — will cause an error in App Router projects
import { useRouter } from "next/router";
```

### @testing-library/jest-dom — NOT installed

This project does NOT have `@testing-library/jest-dom`. All DOM assertions use vitest's built-in matchers:

```typescript
// ✅ Correct — vitest built-in
expect(el).not.toBeNull();
expect(screen.queryByText("some text")).toBeNull();

// ❌ Wrong — jest-dom, will throw "Invalid Chai property"
expect(el).toBeInTheDocument();
```

### useRouter mock pattern for tests

```typescript
const mockPush = vi.fn();

vi.mock("next/navigation", () => ({
  useRouter: () => ({ push: mockPush }),
  notFound: () => { throw new Error("NEXT_NOT_FOUND"); }, // if needed
}));
```

### tsconfig — resolveJsonModule already enabled

`tsconfig.json` has `"resolveJsonModule": true`. No tsconfig changes needed for the JSON import.

### GraphCanvas props reference (do not change)

```tsx
<GraphCanvas
  graphData={graphData}          // GraphData | null
  filterEras={[]}                // string[] — empty until Story 1.13
  filterGenres={[]}              // string[] — empty until Story 1.13
  onPivot={handlePivot}          // (mbid: string) => void
  focalArtistName={graphData?.focalArtist.name ?? ""}  // string
  upstreamCount={...}            // number
  downstreamCount={...}          // number
/>
```

Pass the same props in both `page.tsx` (GraphView) and `ArtistGraphView.tsx`.

### Previous story learnings (Story 1.11)

- **ResizeObserver stub** in `src/test/setup.ts` — already in place; all cmdk/Radix component tests benefit from it
- **No `@testing-library/jest-dom`** — use `not.toBeNull()` throughout
- **TopNav mock pattern** in tests: `vi.mock("@/components/nav/TopNav", () => ({ TopNav: ({ onArtistSelect: _o }: { onArtistSelect: unknown }) => <div data-testid="mock-topnav" /> }))` — avoids cmdk and hook setup in smoke tests

## Dev Agent Record

### Debug Log

| Task | Issue | Resolution | Date |
|------|-------|------------|------|
| 1 | `tsx` not in devDependencies | Installed via `npm install --save-dev tsx` before running generate script | 2026-05-27 |
| 7 | Pre-existing flaky live API test failures (wikidata/musicbrainz timeouts) | Not caused by Story 1.12 changes — same behaviour seen in baseline. 153/154 tests pass; 1 flaky live API test excluded per project convention. | 2026-05-27 |

### Completion Notes

**Story 1.12 complete (2026-05-27)**

- **Task 1**: Created `scripts/generate-landing-data.ts` with full error handling, progress logging, and `mkdirSync` safety. Added `tsx` as devDependency. Added `generate:landing` npm script. Ran script against live MusicBrainz API (completed in <60s). Generated `public/data/miles-davis.json` with 8 artists, 7 edges, 0 warnings — valid GraphData structure with cross-genre connections (Miles Davis jazz → Charlie Puth pop, Gloria Gaynor disco).

- **Task 2**: Replaced `src/app/page.tsx` temp page with real landing. Static JSON imported via `@/../public/data/miles-davis.json` cast to `GraphData`. `useArtistGraph(focalArtistId)` is disabled on landing (query `enabled: !!mbid`). `graphData` is static milesData when `focalArtistId === null`. Loading/error guards both keyed on `focalArtistId &&`. "Follow the thread." subtitle renders with `!focalArtistId` guard, `pointer-events-none z-40` at `top-12`.

- **Task 3**: Created `src/app/artist/[slug]/page.tsx` as Next.js 16 Server Component. Awaits `params` Promise, calls `resolveSlug(slug)`, catches `ArtistNotFoundError` → `notFound()` (HTTP 404 automatic, returns `never`), rethrows other errors.

- **Task 4**: Created `src/app/artist/[slug]/ArtistGraphView.tsx` as `"use client"` named export. Syncs Zustand via `useEffect(() => setFocalArtist(mbid), [mbid, setFocalArtist])`. Uses `useRouter` from `next/navigation` for `handleArtistSelect` and `handlePivot`. `setFocalArtist` is NOT called in either handler (ownership belongs to the receiving component's `useEffect`). `countByDirection` duplicated locally.

- **Task 5**: Created `src/app/artist/[slug]/not-found.tsx` — `"use client"` default export. Uses `useRouter` for artist navigation. Full-height layout with TopNav + centered error text using `&apos;` for apostrophe.

- **Task 6**: Created `src/app/not-found.tsx` — global 404 fallback with same pattern, generic text.

- **Task 7**: Created 4 smoke tests in `src/app/artist/[slug]/not-found.test.tsx`. All 4 pass. Mocks: `next/navigation` (captures `mockPush`), `@/components/nav/TopNav` (captures `onArtistSelect` callback). Tests cover: message text, prop wiring, navigation call, helper text. All assertions use `not.toBeNull()`.

**Test count**: 154 total (150 baseline + 4 new). 153 pass, 1 flaky pre-existing live API timeout.
